items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Andrew Wiggins declining his player option and re-signing on a long-term deal at a lower starting salary could create enough apron space to re-sign Norman Powell.

But after seeing some of today's trades and contracts, Wiggins' market could be too strong for that to be feasible.

The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft pick situations to a slight surplus of first-round picks, mostly in the 2030s.

The 2030 swap with the Heat could potentially offset the swap they already owe to the Trail Blazers.

They could really use more second-round picks.

items.title: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade.

They could trade their 2029 first-round pick on the condition that their 2027 first-round pick (top-14 protected) conveys to Charlotte.

They could try to regain control of it in a trade with the Hornets.

items.title: The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team Giannis Antetokounmpo trade framework.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team Giannis Antetokounmpo trade framework.

They'd create a massive $25.5 million trade exception, which could grow if they expand the trade.

It would be useful for taking on bad salaries with draft picks to add to their surplus.

items.title: Milwaukee got a lot but I have no idea if that Miami deal scares who it needs to. Knicks scared? No. Celtics? Nah - maybe anger them b/c apparently they had a proposed deal per Shams. Cleveland and Detroit? Eye don't think so
items.author: Evan Barnes
items.body_excerpt:
Milwaukee got a lot but I have no idea if that Miami deal scares who it needs to. Knicks scared? No. Celtics? Nah - maybe anger them b/c apparently they had a proposed deal per Shams. Cleveland and Detroit? Eye don't think so

Let's see what happens when the ball rolls out.

items.title: "It's done, it's dead" (c) Doechii.
items.author: Evan Barnes
items.body_excerpt:
"It's done, it's dead" (c) Doechii. 

No more trade rumors or speculation. My Bucks and Heat colleagues can breathe again like Toni Braxton...and get ready for another wild chapter.
